ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Continued To May-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Benjamin Eagleton for Marcel Dubonnet, Administrator (via Zoom).

The court noted that at the previous hearing, Mr. Eagleton had indicated he intended to file a petition to terminate the estate administration for lack of assets under Probate Code section 12251. Mr. Eagleton reported that the petition is now signed and ready for filing and requested a hearing date on the petition prior to June to avoid triggering a new bond premium obligation.

The Court approved the request and authorized May 21, 2025 as the hearing date for the petition. Mr. Eagleton was instructed to make note of this authorization when he submits his petition through e-filing.

The hearing on status of close of estate was continued to May 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Continued To Apr-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Benjamin Eagleton appeared via zoom for Marcel Dubonnet (petitioner).

Mr. Eagelton stated that he is finalizing a settlement agreement that will allow him to file a petition to terminate for lack of assets subject to administration. The matter is continued to April 07, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Continued To Feb-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Benjamin Eagleton appeared by zoom for Marcel Dubonnet.

Mr. Eagleton stated that they have been in touch with the sole beneficiary, that they are working together to resolve the dispute with the creditor and to work this out, and that they will then dismiss the estate. Status on close of estate is continued to February 3, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Eagleton is to file a status report, a petition for final distribution or a dismissal two weeks prior.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-24-2024 Continued To Aug-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Benjamin Eagleton appearing via zoom for Marcel Dubonnet (Petitioner).
Janet Bossenecker appearing via zoom for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated it is not comfortable appointing a creditor and that it does not think the creditor has priority over the public administrator under Probate code 8461 and 8462. Mrs. Bossenecker stated that there are no probate assets, that she thinks the only asset is a half interest in 711 10th street and that she believes that the property is held in two trusts. Mr. Eagleton stated that Francine is collecting rents, that his client has not seen anything, and that his client does not know what the assets are but is not opposed to the public administrator being appointed. Mrs. Bossennecker stated that there is a trust and a trustee, that if Mr. Dubonnet wants to sue the trustee it should be done in a trust not in probate and that the public administrator has zero interest in getting involved in this. The Court excuses Mrs. Bossenecker, thanked her for her time in reviewing this matter, informed Mr. Eagleton that it is not sure of how this is going to play out and that when his client is appointed as administrator, he can't settle his own claim. Mr. Eagleton stated that the beneficiary of the special needs trust passed away and that his client is now a residual beneficiary. The Petition for letters of administration is taken under submission.

Judge:Ross Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not reported

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-01-2023 Continued To May-22-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Continued To May-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Benjamin Eagleton appeared by zoom for Marcel Dubonnet (petitioner).

The court noted that there is no notice to the Public Administrator. Mr. Eagleton stated that upon further review his client is the sibling of the decedent and has a higher priority over a creditor, that he doesn't believe his client has a personal interest in this estate, and that he will provide adequate notice to the Public Administrator. The court noted that the response of October 6th was not verified by the petitioner. Mr. Eagleton stated that he will have his client verify the response, and that he will file a further response to clear the examiner's notes. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to May 1, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Continued To Mar-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom: Benjamin H. Eagleton, Esq. for Marcel Dubonnet (Petitioner/Proposed Administrator).

The Court stated that none of the probate examiner's notes were addressed. Mr. Eagleton stated that this was an unusual administration, that his client has claims against his sister, that his client is tied to two special needs trusts, that his client filed a creditor's claim and thinks that he does have a right to act, that the public administrator would need to decline, that the decedent's daughter has not responded, and that she did not lodge a will. The Court noted that the decedent's daughter may not understand her obligation to lodge the will. The Court and counsel briefly confer on how best to proceed. Mr. Eagleton suggested that he could do another notice to appear or that a citation could issue. The Court ordered Mr. Eagleton to address the issues raised in the examiner's notes including addressing item 3f(1) of the petition and the bond. The Court also ordered Mr. Eagleton to give notice to the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA). Mr. Eagleton stated that he will clear the examiner's notice and provide notice to the SFPA.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.
